name: Will installing a new exterior door make my home more energy efficient?
headline: Will installing a new exterior door make my home more energy efficient?

mainEntity.name: Will installing a new exterior door make my home more energy efficient?
mainEntity.text: Will installing a new exterior door make my home more energy efficient?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes, upgrading to a better exterior door can improve your home’s energy efficiency, especially in cooler and windier areas like the coastal Peninsula. Modern doors often have insulated cores, tighter seals, and improved thresholds that help block drafts and reduce heat loss. If your current door is old, warped, or has gaps you can see light through, it’s probably letting in cold air and moisture. A new, well-installed door with proper weatherstripping and foam insulation around the frame can make rooms near the entry noticeably more comfortable. Look for a door rated for energy performance, with good insulation values and quality construction. Glass inserts and sidelights should be double-pane and properly sealed. Even small improvements, like upgrading the sweep at the bottom and tightening the threshold, can help. Pitfalls include assuming that just buying an “energy-efficient” door is enough. Poor installation can undo most of the benefits. If the frame isn’t sealed and insulated, air and water can still sneak in around the edges. Also, some very cheap doors claim efficiency but use thin materials or low-quality weatherstripping that wears out quickly. To get the best results, think of the door as part of a system: the slab, frame, weatherstripping, threshold, and surrounding wall all need to work together. That way, you get comfort, lower energy use, and a door that stands up to coastal weather.
